reviews.review_text:
I’m sure it will be fabulous just like it says 😊 and many people happily confirm
I’m yet to experience it myself as my booking is coming up 🙏👍
Kristina is best for communication & I honestly give her 10 Stars
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
For customer service and support and if you need booking, WOTIF is confusing and not accurate to say at least.
Looking forward to my birthday in this wonderful location and everything that has to offer.👍👍👍

What a great bush camp, well organised and clean. Showers are bucket type and great fun to use.
We done the nocturnal tour with host Paul, definitely worth it and very interesting.

We camped here over Halloween and had the BEST time! Our host Krystina put on a fantastic Halloween shindig with decorations galore, amazing yummy treats, a big bonfire, and a kids movie night. She was lovely, always had time for the kids and let them help her with the decorations, and was always available for a chat. Close to visit Talia Caves. Bucket showers were lots of fun. Camp area was huge with plenty of space for kids to ride bikes. But the amazing host really made the whole experience.

Had the pleasure of spending 2 peaceful nights camping here. Huge camping area, cute swag huts & dinky bucket showers! Also has a well set out camp kitchen. Highly recommend staying & we will go back.

Such a unique experience! The bucket-showers were so creative and fun to use, and the location couldn’t be better, just a few minutes from Talia Caves. We even spotted dolphins at nearby Venus Bay, which made our stay extra special. Highly recommend for anyone who prefers bush camping to caravan parks.
We camped overnight. The facilities were very unloved and looked as though they needed more maintenance and cleaning. Disappointed.

Absolutely beautiful owners
So friendly,
Lots of nature around, beach so close
This camp was amazing to stay at. Time and effort has definitely  been put into this place to keep it looking great. Very friendly hosts. Very tidy camping areas and facilities.

Our 4x4 club had the pleasure of staying at this absolutely amazing and gorgeous place.
As we are completely off grid campers, it was the perfect setting for our band of enthusiasts.
If you're wanting some conveniences, there is a communal camp kitchen, toilets and bucket showers.  Complete with hot water.
Which some of our group did utilised.
The service was above and beyond our expectations.
Christine and Paul were there to help with anything from directions, local fishing and tourist spots, even allowing us to pat their fur babies.
Nothing was too hard and always with a smile and a joke.
Would highly recommend anyone heading along the South Australian coast to stay a night or 10.
We will be back, without a doubt.

We stayed at the Piccidilly cottage just for a night as we were driving through to Perth. It would have been good to enjoy the outback retreat for a couple of days. The wifi and tv options were not good , but just could manage as it was just for a night stay.The best part was the Deep Space Observatory we got to enjoy completely free Thanks to PK Imaging it was an awesome experience we had . Hats off to his passion for Deep Space. The was the highlight of our stay.

Lovely camp spot and guests
Close to awesome beaches and Venus Bay

We stayed here for an extended period over Christmas and New year. We enjoyed the experience as a whole, the campsites where great with plenty of space, the camp kitchen was pretty good hot running water etc, the bucket shower was a good experience when finding a clean one out of about 10, these could be washed thoroughly on a weekly basis, there was a lot of gunk in them that wouldn't be too healthy. Having garbage and recycling disposals was also a massive plus!

For eco camping , this is great. Swag huts and camping spots. Plenty of room. Camp kitchen. Drop toilet - it is eco and a bucket shower. Tracks to beaches and caves - so quiet and easy to check in.  There are cabins as well.

Lovely cabins and campsites to stay in (tried out both) and the couple that runs the place are the best - so helpful and kind !! Would definitely recommend staying there and checking out their gorgeous private beach ! We will definitely be going back :))

Not a fan of this campground, camp kitchen need to be clean, toilets was smelly and I don’t know what is going wrong but the water from shower block tap smells awful…
The thing I like are the sites and kitchen was large and very quiet peaceful.

Came here with the family and it was brilliant. Beautiful location and awesome fun over the holidays.

Highly recommend

what an absolutly fun filled week we spent at coodlie Park, kids had a ball, Bon fires, the lambs and wildlife, Paul taking kids on night walks and saving lambs, he was so educational. we had coal fire dinner on the cliffs watching the sunset followed by star gazing, Kristina was so knowledgeable about the sky. we stayed in a cabin and it was equipped with everything we needed. nice and clean and bed was comfy. 10/10 best part was kids wernt even bothered about ipads. we are already planning our trip back before December! kids are counting down, we can't wait, coffee on the balcony with beautiful view.. bliss. thanks Paul and Kristina 💚
Great place to stay.  We really enjoyed our time there.  Very friendly and helpful owners.

I can testify from experience that Camp Coodlie is great. Usually bush camping means "there is the bush, camp anywhere". Camp Coodlie is SO MUCH MORE, from the elevated swag huts, and the spacious camp kitchen and dining hall, to the funky hot water bucket showers. And you are in the bush, with absolutely no light pollution all the way to the horizon in every direction.|I was hosted in January and again in May 2024 for a week each time to provide live astronomy Deep Space Theatre shows as community events.  Their enthusiasm and support adds so much to the travellers' experiences and to their local communities.
Awesome spot!

Lovely, friendly & informative hosts. We took our caravan and camped at the bush site. Visited Woolshed Caves, The Tub and Tahlia Beach. Drove out to the sunset spot overlooking the Ocean, unbelievable sunset.

We recently returned from Coodlie Park Eco Retreat, and it was absolutely the perfect spot to unwind. The hosts, Christina and Paul, were exceptionally warm and inviting, setting a fantastic tone from the start.

The camping area offers various places to set up a tent, and they've cleverly provided shelters specifically designed for swags. The highlight for many would be the camp kitchen—spacious enough to whip up large meals and with seating for 30-40 people, making it an ideal gathering spot to meet other travellers.

During our stay, it was quiet with few other guests, giving us a sense of having the expansive place to ourselves. The children had a blast, with plenty of room to cycle and explore without worries.

The retreat is conveniently located near Venus Bay and Elliston, making it a great base for beach excursions and visiting nearby attractions. We stayed from April 16th to 25th and truly took advantage of the proximity to local sights.

Christina and Paul have created a truly special retreat that’s both relaxing and kid-friendly amidst beautiful surroundings. For those looking for a peaceful retreat where kids can freely enjoy themselves, Coodlie Park is an excellent choice.

Overall: It comes highly recommended for anyone wanting a family holiday or just to relax and connect with nature.

Our family were welcomed with open arms by the owners here.  A tour of the properties local cliff lined beach, a guided 4wd tour to the sunset spot and visits to the “tub” - collapsed caves and local sights.|Our daughter was thrilled to play with the owner’s niece and to pet their few day old baby “abandoned” sheep at the homestead.|Really liked the eco philosophy behind this rustic retreat which the owners encourage.

From the moment that we arrived, we throughly enjoyed our stay at Coodlie Park. This is bush camping at its best and has to be one of the best bush camping that we have experienced. The rustic camp kitchen was well equipped and we loved using the hot water bucket showers which added to the bush experience. Plenty of open spaces to camp, park the caravan and have fires. It was clean and well maintained everyday. Hosts Kyrstina and Paul were very friendly and welcoming with lots of information to share about the area. The sunset views are amazing. Can't wait to return to create more unforgettable memories.
Nice and quite bushcamp close to Mt Camel Beach, The Woolshed and Talia Beach

Wonderful spot. Plenty of space to camp so you're nowhere near others, nice facilities to cook and clean in. Family that run the place are extremely friendly, the sweats available at the office are a must-try. Nice little 4WD bush track to the Talia Caves.

Great camp ground with massive potential. It is more suited for people who want to camp, rather than people with caravans. "You can park you van wherever you like but please don't park in front of the camping huts, we have 22 acres availble". The map for the park shows were all the huts are located. They are great, easy to drive too, but when towing a van, with no real direction, you are left wondering where you can park and if you can turn around or will you get stuck. We have a fully self contained caravan, but we still wanted to be near the toilets, for number 2s and experience the bucket showers. It would be a huge benefit if the park had a map which included cleared spaces for vans. As most spots caravans would need to park in long grass which isn't ideal. The beach access isn't to a "easy access beach" the road was pretty rough, some overgrown bushes scratching the car and a steep decline in foot down a cliff to the beach. With a bit more planning for vans, not just people in a tent, this place would be great. Located near Talia caves, which were amazing. Staff on check in were friendly.
Such a beautiful camp ground. Well maintained, great spot and the owners were so lovely even managed to taste one of their homemade cupcakes. Couldn't ask for a better time.
